name: US Executive Order on Safe, Secure, and Trustworthy AI (EO 14110)
summary: Executive Order 14110 established the most comprehensive US federal AI governance framework, requiring safety testing and reporting for dual-use foundation models, directing federal agencies to develop AI governance policies, and addressing risks from AI in critical infrastructure, cybersecurity, and biological weapons. Note: portions were rescinded by subsequent executive action in January 2025.
keyRequirements: Safety testing and red-teaming reporting for dual-use foundation models exceeding compute thresholds
NIST AI Risk Management Framework and AI safety standards development
Federal agency AI governance policies and Chief AI Officer designations
Watermarking and content authentication for AI-generated content
Privacy protections for AI training data and federal data practices
Civil rights protections against AI-enabled discrimination in housing, lending, and employment
Workforce impact assessments and AI talent recruitment for federal government
penalties: Compliance is mandatory for federal agencies and contractors through existing regulatory authorities. Dual-use foundation model developers must report safety test results under the Defense Production Act. Non-compliance may result in loss of federal contracts and regulatory action under applicable sector-specific laws.
